message:
fix(viewer,graph): add deferred callback tracking, force graph teardown on route exit, and idempotent bridge listeners

- Add scheduleTimeout/clearScheduledTimeout/clearScheduledTimeouts to
  graph.js, routing all setTimeout calls through a tracked Set so
  cleanup() can cancel pending callbacks that would fire after teardown
- Add graphBridgeListenersBound flag preventing duplicate bridge listeners
- Add teardownForceGraph() called on route exit from graph view
- Update route transitions to clear graphDetailNode on both issue-detail
  and issue-list transitions separately
- Add tests asserting teardown, bridge listener idempotency, and tracked
  timeout cleanup ordering
- Sync beads issue tracker and history snapshots

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-24mj","title":"Epic: Make analysis thresholds and weights configurable","description":"The analysis engine contains numerous hard-coded numeric constants that serve as algorithm parameters: scoring weights, truncation limits, staleness thresholds, drift detection percentages, and search bonuses. While these are intentional design decisions tuned for Go legacy parity (not bugs), they represent policy choices that different teams may want to customize.

## Current State
All parameters are hard-coded as const values or inline literals. They are tuned to match the Go bv tool behavior and produce correct conformance test results (78/78 passing). Changing them would break conformance.

## Why This Is P3 (Not Urgent)
1. Current values produce correct, Go-compatible output
2. No users have requested configurability
3. The README explicitly describes these as the scoring model design
4. Exposing them prematurely adds complexity without demand

## When To Promote
- When real users request different weightings for their workflows
- When the project moves beyond Go parity into Rust-native territory
- When the recommendation feedback system (--feedback-accept/ignore) matures enough to auto-tune weights

## Inventory of Parameters (from code audit)
See subtasks for categorized lists with file/line references.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:05:42.723654703Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:51:04.423976391Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:51:04.423437682Z","close_reason":"All 5 subtasks complete: weights, limits, alerts, drift, search","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["analysis","configurability"]}
 {"id":"bd-24mj.1","title":"Make triage scoring weights configurable via config file or CLI","description":"The 8-component impact score in triage.rs uses hard-coded weights:

  W_PAGERANK: 0.22
  W_BETWEENNESS: 0.20
  W_BLOCKER_RATIO: 0.13
  W_STALENESS: 0.05
  W_PRIORITY_BOOST: 0.10
  W_TIME_TO_IMPACT: 0.10
  W_URGENCY: 0.10
  W_RISK: 0.10

These are defined at src/analysis/triage.rs lines 14-21. They sum to 1.0 and are designed to balance structural importance (PageRank, betweenness) against operational signals (priority, urgency, staleness).

DESIGN CONSIDERATIONS:
- Weights must sum to 1.0 — validation required
- Must not break conformance tests (78/78) — probably needs a 'legacy' preset
- Could be exposed via .bv/config.yaml or --weight-preset CLI flag
- Presets like 'default', 'graph-heavy', 'priority-first', 'quick-wins' would be more useful than raw number tuning
- The recommendation feedback system (--feedback-accept/ignore) could eventually auto-tune these

APPROACH:
1. Define a WeightPreset enum with named configurations
2. Add a --weight-preset flag to CliArgs
3. Load custom weights from .bv/config.yaml if present
4. Validate sum-to-1.0 invariant
5. Pass weights through TriageOptions to compute_triage()
6. Add conformance test for default preset matching Go behavior

URGENCY SCORES (lines 176-183) should also be part of this:
  in_progress: 1.0, open: 0.8, review: 0.7, blocked: 0.5, deferred: 0.3, default: 0.6

STALENESS NORMALIZATION CAP (line 150): 90 days — should be part of preset.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","estimated_minutes":120,"created_at":"2026-03-25T03:06:01.251658150Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:37:17.954828389Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:37:17.954198637Z","close_reason":"WeightPreset enum with 5 named presets, --weight-preset CLI flag, 4 new tests. Composes with feedback adjustments. Conformance 78/78. (commit 058636d)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["analysis","configurability","triage"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-24mj.1","depends_on_id":"bd-24mj","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:06:01.251658150Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-24mj.1","depends_on_id":"bd-hpt8.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:08:52.813088349Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-3qa","title":"Infrastructure unit tests + integration tests","description":"WHAT: Test coverage for config, cache, feedback, and async. (1) Config tests: AnalysisConfig loads from YAML file, from env var, from CLI flag. Merge with defaults works (missing fields get defaults). Invalid config rejected with clear error. Disabled metrics actually skipped. Timeout enforcement works (mock slow computation). Size thresholds respected. (2) Cache tests: cache hit returns same result. Cache miss computes fresh. TTL expiry works (fast-forward time). Graph change invalidates cache (hash differs). Thread-safe concurrent access. Memory cap enforced. (3) Feedback tests: feedback.json round-trips correctly. Score deltas apply in correct direction. Missing file = no feedback (not error). Corrupt file = clear error message. (4) Async tests: fast metrics return immediately. Slow metrics arrive eventually (use channel). TUI poll picks up completed results. Graph size below threshold runs synchronously. (5) E2e script (scripts/test_infra_parity.sh): end-to-end config → cache → feedback → triage pipeline with verbose logging at each stage. LOGGING: Every test logs config state, cache state (hits/misses/evictions), feedback state, timing info. ACCEPTANCE: All infrastructure modules have >80% coverage. E2e pipeline script passes. PARENT: bd-3in","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-08T00:26:52.813031109Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-09T21:47:59.119746830Z","closed_at":"2026-03-09T21:47:59.119318818Z","close_reason":"Added 18 infrastructure unit tests covering: AnalysisConfig preset validation (full/triage_only/fast_phase/slow_phase/for_size — flag correctness, boundary at 10k nodes, default=full), disabled metric recording and skipped_metrics tracking, empty graph metrics, merge idempotence, has_pending_slow_metrics lifecycle, two-phase integration (fast→triage→slow→improved triage, fast+slow=full insights), selective config via new_with_config, empty issues resilience for all operations (triage/insights/plan/spawn). Total: 1154 tests, 0 failures.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3qa","depends_on_id":"bd-3dy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T00:27:42.064956575Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3qa","depends_on_id":"bd-3ud","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T00:27:40.570153763Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3qa","depends_on_id":"bd-4h6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T00:27:39.280039793Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3qa","depends_on_id":"bd-6gv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T00:27:38.480328883Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":50,"issue_id":"bd-3qa","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"TESTING STRATEGY: Infrastructure tests need both unit isolation AND integration. Unit: test each component in isolation with mocks. Integration: test the full pipeline (config → cache → analysis → feedback → triage). The cache TTL test needs a way to fast-forward time — either make the clock injectable or use a short TTL (100ms) and sleep. The async test needs a way to control when 'slow' computation completes — use a channel/barrier pattern.","created_at":"2026-03-08T00:28:28Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3tj","title":"Implement rich Graph/History TUI views on frankentui","description":"Replace placeholder graph/history modes with data-rich panes, keyboard parity, and snapshot tests.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-02-17T23:42:20.004189992Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-18T01:28:41.770292168Z","closed_at":"2026-02-18T01:28:41.770272431Z","close_reason":"Replaced graph/history placeholders with data-rich panes, added navigation parity and snapshot-like tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["frankentui","graph","history","parity","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3tj","depends_on_id":"bd-1if","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-04T23:11:35Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3tj","depends_on_id":"bd-2p3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-04T23:11:35Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3ud","title":"Feedback loop: persistent scoring adjustments","description":"WHAT: Implement a feedback mechanism where users can adjust triage scores (e.g. 'this issue is more important than the algorithm thinks') and those adjustments persist across sessions. WHY: Go has adjustScore() in pkg/analysis/triage.go that reads from .beads/feedback.json to apply user overrides to computed triage scores. This is the 'human-in-the-loop' piece that makes the triage engine actually useful over time — pure algorithmic scoring misses domain context. IMPLEMENTATION: Create src/analysis/feedback.rs. FeedbackStore struct loads/saves .beads/feedback.json (or BV_FEEDBACK_FILE env var). Each entry: {issue_id, score_delta, reason, timestamp}. Wire into triage scoring: after computing algorithmic score, apply any feedback deltas. Expose via --robot-feedback CLI flag for reading current feedback, and a TUI keybinding (f) for inline adjustment. ACCEPTANCE: Feedback persists in JSON file. Score adjustments apply on next triage run. --robot-feedback outputs current feedback state. TUI shows adjusted scores with visual indicator. DEPENDS ON: bd-ybj.2 (TriageScoringOptions — need the scoring infrastructure to hook into). PARENT: bd-3in","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-08T00:03:02.426671598Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-09T01:27:56.713485744Z","closed_at":"2026-03-09T01:27:56.711642092Z","close_reason":"Wired feedback weight adjustments into triage scoring pipeline. FeedbackData.weight_adjustment_map() produces HashMap for TriageScoringOptions.weight_adjustments. compute_impact_score() applies multipliers with renormalization. All 7 main.rs triage sites updated. 3 unit tests added (shift, empty baseline, renormalize). All 713 tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3ud","depends_on_id":"bd-ybj.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T00:04:42.195024539Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":44,"issue_id":"bd-3ud","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"DESIGN: feedback.json format: [{issue_id: 'bd-xxx', delta: 0.3, reason: 'domain expert priority', timestamp: '2026-...', author: 'user@host'}]. Load on triage startup, apply deltas after algorithmic scoring. TUI 'f' key opens inline editor: up/down to adjust delta, Enter to confirm, text field for reason. DEPENDS ON bd-ybj.2 for the scoring hook point.","created_at":"2026-03-08T00:09:21Z"},{"id":55,"issue_id":"bd-3ud","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CORRECTION: Previous description referenced 'adjustScore()' which doesn't exist. The actual Go function is RecordFeedback() in feedback.go line 125, signature: func (f *FeedbackData) RecordFeedback(issueID, action string, score float64, breakdown ScoreBreakdown) error. Actions are 'accept' or 'ignore'. Weight adjustments use exponential smoothing with multipliers in 0.5-2.0 range. Feedback events are stored as FeedbackEvent structs with issue_id, action, score, timestamp. The weights adjusted are for all 8 ScoreBreakdown components: PageRank, Betweenness, BlockerRatio, Staleness, PriorityBoost, TimeToImpact, Urgency, Risk.","created_at":"2026-03-08T00:33:23Z"},{"id":97,"issue_id":"bd-3ud","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"TESTING REQUIREMENT: This task is NOT complete until it includes inline unit tests. Config: test load/merge/default. Cache: test hit/miss/TTL/invalidation. Feedback: test round-trip/apply/corrupt-file. Async: test fast-return/eventual-complete.","created_at":"2026-03-08T00:37:48Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-4663","title":"Use canonical dashboard route when closing issue modal","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-28T14:13:04.113654428Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-28T14:15:52.990468138Z","closed_at":"2026-03-28T14:15:52.990208967Z","close_reason":"Closing issue modal from dashboard now uses the canonical dashboard route instead of relying on router fallback from #/dashboard.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-4h6","title":"Cache layer: hash-based invalidation with TTL for graph metrics","description":"WHAT: Add a computation cache that stores previously computed graph metrics keyed by a hash of the input graph structure, with configurable TTL expiry. WHY: Go caches analysis results (pkg/analysis/cache.go) so repeated triage/plan/insights calls on unchanged data are near-instant. Our Rust engine recomputes everything from scratch every time. For interactive TUI use, this causes noticeable lag when switching between views. IMPLEMENTATION: Create src/analysis/cache.rs with GraphMetricsCache struct. Key = SHA-256 of sorted (node_ids + edge_pairs). Value = all computed metrics (PageRank scores, betweenness, HITS, etc.). TTL configurable via AnalysisConfig (default 5 minutes). Cache is in-memory only (no disk persistence needed). Wire into GraphAnalyzer so compute_all_metrics checks cache first. ACCEPTANCE: Second call with same graph returns cached results in <1ms. Cache invalidates when graph changes. TTL expiry works. Cache hit/miss stats available for debugging. PARENT: bd-3in","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-08T00:02:49.091260977Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-09T02:23:35.423439122Z","closed_at":"2026-03-09T02:23:35.420164320Z","close_reason":"Created analysis/cache.rs with MetricsCache: SHA-256 keyed in-memory cache with configurable TTL (default 5 min), automatic expired-entry eviction, hit/miss stats. 9 unit tests (hit, miss, status-change invalidation, TTL expiry, clear, deterministic key, config-sensitive key, empty graph, default impl). All 725 tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":43,"issue_id":"bd-4h6","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"DESIGN: Use a simple HashMap<[u8; 32], CachedMetrics> where the key is SHA-256 of the graph structure. CachedMetrics holds all computed values plus a timestamp. On cache check: hash current graph, look up, check TTL. Thread-safe via Arc<RwLock<>>. No disk persistence — cache is session-scoped. Memory bound: cap at ~100 entries (each is small, just numbers).","created_at":"2026-03-08T00:09:17Z"},{"id":56,"issue_id":"bd-4h6","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CORRECTION: Previous description said 'No disk persistence needed.' However, Go cache.go has BOTH in-memory and disk caching (robotAnalysisDiskCacheFileName = 'analysis_cache.json'). For full parity we should implement disk caching too, at minimum for robot outputs. Memory cache uses sync.RWMutex (Rust: Arc<RwLock<>>). Disk cache writes to .beads/analysis_cache.json. Also noted: Go uses a global package-level cache instance (globalCache) — Rust should use a similar static or passed-through cache reference.","created_at":"2026-03-08T00:33:31Z"},{"id":96,"issue_id":"bd-4h6","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"TESTING REQUIREMENT: This task is NOT complete until it includes inline unit tests. Config: test load/merge/default. Cache: test hit/miss/TTL/invalidation. Feedback: test round-trip/apply/corrupt-file. Async: test fast-return/eventual-complete.","created_at":"2026-03-08T00:37:47Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-54fy","title":"Support multi-repo workspace baselines","description":"Currently, --save-baseline and --robot-drift only work for single-repo workspaces. Multi-repo workspaces silently skip baseline operations (src/main.rs:2827-2828).

CURRENT BEHAVIOR:
When a .bv/workspace.yaml has multiple enabled repos, the baseline code skips namespacing and saves/loads a flat baseline. This means:
- Baselines saved from multi-repo workspaces may have ID collisions
- Drift detection against such baselines could produce incorrect results
- No error or warning is emitted — it silently operates on un-namespaced data

WHY THIS IS HARD:
Per-repo baselines require:
1. Splitting the baseline file into per-repo sections (or using a directory of baseline files)
2. Namespacing issue IDs consistently between save and load
3. Handling the case where repos are added/removed between baseline snapshots
4. Deciding whether cross-repo drift (e.g., new dependency edge between repos) should be tracked

APPROACH:
1. Save baselines as a JSON object keyed by repo prefix
2. On load, match each section to the current workspace repos
3. Report removed/added repos as drift events
4. Namespace IDs using the same logic as loader::namespace_workspace_issues()

WORKAROUND: Users with multi-repo workspaces can save baselines per-repo by running bvr with --beads-file pointing to each repo individually.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2026-03-25T03:07:00.870871066Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:59:36.565920481Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:59:36.565121849Z","close_reason":"Added multi-repo workspace warnings for --save-baseline, --check-drift, --robot-drift. Full per-repo baseline support deferred as future feature (commit 8f2856f).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["baseline","drift","workspace"]}
 {"id":"bd-5axg","title":"Restore cargo fmt --check to green by reconciling repo-wide formatting drift","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-26T21:24:14.695695923Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-26T21:26:16.617961989Z","closed_at":"2026-03-26T21:26:16.617703019Z","close_reason":"Normalized repo files so cargo fmt --check, cargo check, and cargo clippy all pass","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-8cf5","title":"Kill timed-out export hooks as a process tree","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:16:43.561120292Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-27T18:20:48.577494611Z","closed_at":"2026-03-27T18:20:48.577230370Z","close_reason":"Made timed-out export hooks terminate their Unix process group so descendants do not keep running after timeout","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-8vgr","title":"Implement --label flag subgraph filtering (Go parity gap)","description":"The --label flag currently only computes label health metadata and includes it in the output envelope (label_scope, label_context fields). It does NOT actually filter the issue graph to the label's subgraph before running analysis.

The Go tool (main.go:1458-1471) calls ComputeLabelSubgraph() which replaces the issues list with only those in the label's connected component, then reruns analysis on the filtered set. This means --robot-insights, --robot-plan, and --robot-priority return label-scoped results in Go but full-graph results in Rust.

IMPACT: When a user runs 'bvr --robot-plan --label backend', they expect to see only backend-related issues in the plan. Currently they get the full plan with a metadata tag saying label_scope=backend.

APPROACH:
1. Add a compute_label_subgraph() function that extracts issues with the given label plus their transitive dependencies
2. In main.rs, after computing label_scope, replace the issues vec and rebuild the analyzer with filtered issues
3. Ensure triage, plan, insights, and priority all operate on the filtered set
4. Add conformance tests for label-scoped output

AFFECTED COMMANDS: --robot-insights, --robot-plan, --robot-priority (as documented in Go --label flag help text)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","estimated_minutes":120,"created_at":"2026-03-26T01:22:46.641365520Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-26T21:03:28.137303801Z","closed_at":"2026-03-26T21:03:28.137109764Z","close_reason":"Implemented compute_label_subgraph() with full connected component walk. Integrated into main.rs to replace analyzer with scoped issues when --label is set. 4 tests, 78/78 conformance. (commit c04fcaa)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["analysis","cli","parity"]}
 {"id":"bd-9f0","title":"Analysis engine unit tests + conformance + e2e","description":"WHAT: Comprehensive test coverage for all analysis engine additions. THREE layers: (1) Unit tests per module — ImpactScore computation with known graph fixtures (verify each of 8 components), TriageScoringOptions weight application, StaleCleanup detection accuracy, WhatIf delta correctness, advanced insights (TopKSet, CoverageSet, KPaths, CycleBreak) against hand-calculated expected results. (2) Conformance tests: generate Go reference fixtures for triage scoring on shared test graphs, compare Rust output. (3) E2e script (scripts/test_analysis_parity.sh) that builds test .beads/ directories with known issue graphs, runs both bv --robot-triage and bvr --robot-triage, compares scoring order and component breakdown. LOGGING: Every test should log the input graph structure, expected scores, actual scores, and delta. On failure, dump the full graph and all intermediate metric values. FIXTURES: Create at least 3 test graphs — (a) simple linear chain (5 nodes), (b) complex with cycles and clusters (20+ nodes), (c) edge case with single node and empty graph. ACCEPTANCE: All analysis modules have >90% line coverage. Conformance fixtures match. E2e script passes. PARENT: bd-ybj","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-08T00:25:55.273518217Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-09T18:26:01.825812552Z","closed_at":"2026-03-09T18:26:01.825309139Z","close_reason":"Added 75 new analysis unit tests: suggest.rs (22 new: duplicates, missing deps, labels, cycles, helpers), forecast.rs (20 new: median, complexity, velocity, confidence, agents), alerts.rs (19 new: stale thresholds, in_progress multiplier, cascade severity, filters, summarize), label_intel.rs (18 new: velocity, freshness, flow, criticality, composite health). Fixed 2 snapshot tests. Every test should use deterministic graph fixtures (no randomness). Use property-based testing where possible (e.g., all ImpactScore components sum to total, WhatIf deltas are symmetric). The 3-graph fixture set (linear, complex, edge-case) should be shared across all analysis tests. Go conformance may not be exact due to floating-point — use epsilon comparison (1e-6).","created_at":"2026-03-08T00:28:13Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-aeae","title":"Make BACKGROUND_THRESHOLD configurable for graph analysis phasing","description":"In src/analysis/graph.rs line 256, BACKGROUND_THRESHOLD is hard-coded to 200:

  pub const BACKGROUND_THRESHOLD: usize = 200;

This determines when the analyzer spawns a background thread for expensive metrics (betweenness, HITS, eigenvector, k-core). Below 200 nodes, everything runs synchronously.

WHY 200:
The Go tool uses a similar threshold. At 200 nodes, betweenness centrality (O(V*E)) starts taking noticeable time. Below that, sync computation is fine.

WHEN TO CHANGE:
- Fast machines could handle 500+ nodes synchronously
- Slow machines or CI might want 100
- Users with --force-full-analysis already bypass the phasing entirely

APPROACH:
- Add to .bv/config.yaml as experimental.background_threshold
- Default to 200 for backward compatibility
- Document in --robot-help output","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","estimated_minutes":30,"created_at":"2026-03-25T03:07:11.999859406Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:48:52.282375274Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:48:52.281860760Z","close_reason":"background_threshold() reads BVR_BACKGROUND_THRESHOLD env var (commit 70885a0)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["analysis","configurability","performance"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-aeae","depends_on_id":"bd-24mj.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:09:08.668984122Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
